title: US-India trade deal news: 'There's a handful of issues that remain' - What Sergio Gor revealed
description: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor expressed optimism that the deal would be finalised in the coming weeks or months, stating that the negotiations have advanced at an unusually rapid pace compared with many major trade agreements.
content: US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor said that India and the United States are on the verge of securing a bilateral trade pact, with only "a handful of issues" left to resolve, as negotiators shift their focus toward finalising the legal language before both nations sign the agreement.In an exclusive conversation with IANS at the White House, Gor expressed optimism that the deal would be finalised in the coming weeks or months, stating that the negotiations have advanced at an unusually rapid pace compared with many major trade agreements."A lot of it is the language that has to be written," Gor said, referring to the remaining work on the agreement. "I was in those meetings, that was 48 hours ago, with (US Trade Representative) Ambassador Jamieson Greer in Delhi, and we met with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal, who's a great friend of mine. It was very productive,” he said."There's a handful of issues that remain. A lot of it now is on the language that ultimately both sides will sign," he added."We're confident that over the next few weeks, over the next few months, it'll get done,” Gor further said.Related News | Goldman Sachs lifts India's CY26 GDP growth forecast after US-Iran deal; cuts CAD estimates - DetailsGor emphasized that the timeline of these negotiations should be kept in context, pointing out that discussions for the proposed US-India agreement have been underway for only roughly 18 months. "Look, to put it in perspective, we've been working on this deal for a year and a half," he said. "The European Union deal, which is still not done, is 20 years. Everybody says, 'Why is this taking so long?' We're on an incredible trajectory of getting it done,” Gor said.While choosing not to disclose the contents of the proposed agreement, Gor maintained that both Washington and New Delhi were working towards an outcome that would benefit each side."I don't want to reveal too much. You're going to have to wait and see," he said. "It's one of those things when you find common ground, and we're able to identify things that are good for each side, that's when the deal happens."The US Ambassador also noted that President Donald Trump remains eager about visiting India, following his recent interaction with Prime Minister Narendra Modi in France."I don't have exact dates yet," Gor said. "I just left the President. I was with him for several hours in the Oval Office.""One of the things the President asked about is, 'So when am I coming?' He's very keen to come. The Prime Minister invited him. I think that'll happen at some point,” he said.Though he decline from providing a timeline due to the upcoming US midterm election cycle, Gor stated that India is positioned high among the President's global travel priorities."We have midterms coming up here in the United States, so the President has a very hectic and busy schedule focusing on domestic travel. But with that said, India's high on the list of places where he'll visit soon,” he said.Gor underscored that the personal relationship between US President Trump and PM Modi serves as strong foundation for bilateral ties despite periodic speculation about strains in the relationship. "I think we're in a great place," he said. "One of the big anchors to that relationship is the relationship between the President and the Prime Minister that has always remained strong,” he added."The President and Prime Minister are great friends, and that's something that goes back years ago, and it's something that will continue to go years ahead,” Gor stated.India and the United States continue to hammer out the foundational stage of a bilateral trade pact, which is calibrated to lower tariff walls, enhance market access, and fortify economic integration. Both administrations have repeatedly framed the agreement as a vital economic priority, aiming to cement this primary framework before transitioning to a more extensive trade paradigm.Trade and commerce have quickly evolved into one of the most fastest-growing pillars of the US-India strategic alliance, which also spans defence, technology, critical and emerging technologies, energy, education and people-to-people ties. The two countries have increasingly prioritised economic integration alongside their expanding strategic cooperation across the vital Indo-Pacific region.

title: পত্নীক চোকা অস্ত্ৰৰে হত্যাৰ চেষ্টা স্বামীৰ...
Batwara 1947: How Aamir Khan Productions, Sunny Deol and Preity Zinta bring Asghar Wajahat's reportedly Pakistan-banned partition play to big screen
description: Aamir Khan Productions' Batwara 1947, starring Sunny Deol and Preity Zinta, adapts Asghar Wajahat's acclaimed Partition play Jis Lahore Nai Vekhya. The historical drama explores humanity, displacement and resilience through a story reportedly banned in Pakistan.
content: When India marks Independence every year, the celebrations are filled with stories of freedom, courage and nation-building. But hidden beneath the triumphant narrative lies another story—one of abandoned homes, broken families, impossible choices and friendships tested by newly drawn borders. It is a story that cinema has only occasionally dared to tell. That is precisely where Batwara 1947 steps in. Produced by Aamir Khan Productions and directed by National Award-winning filmmaker Rajkumar Santoshi, the period drama, starring Sunny Deol, Preity Zinta, Shabana Azmi, Karan Deol and Ali Fazal, promises to revisit one of the darkest chapters of the subcontinent's history—not through politicians or soldiers, but through ordinary people whose lives were forever altered by Partition. Yet what makes the film even more fascinating is not just its subject. It is the remarkable journey of the story that inspired it—a celebrated stage play that has crossed borders, sparked conversations and, according to reports, even faced a ban in Pakistan. Why does a 37-year-old play still resonate today? The foundation of Batwara 1947 lies in Professor Asghar Wajahat's acclaimed 1989 play Jis Lahore Nai Vekhya, O Jamya E Nai—a Punjabi phrase that loosely translates to "You haven't truly lived until you've seen Lahore." Rather than narrating Partition through statistics or political speeches, the play focuses on a single haveli in Lahore. Sometimes, history is best understood through one front door instead of an entire map. The story follows Sikander Mirza, a Muslim refugee forced to leave Lucknow after Partition. His family is allotted an elegant haveli abandoned by a Hindu family that fled to India. But the house is not empty. An elderly Hindu woman—simply called Mai—has refused to leave. What begins as an uncomfortable standoff gradually evolves into something far more powerful: a relationship that transcends religion, nationality and politics. As distrust melts into affection, the haveli becomes a symbol of what the newly divided nations had lost—the ability to see one another simply as human beings. Was Partition really only about borders? History often remembers 1947 as the year India and Pakistan were born. Less frequently discussed is the staggering human cost. The Partition triggered one of the largest forced migrations in recorded history. Historians estimate that between 14 and 18 million people crossed the newly created borders in a matter of months. Families left behind homes, businesses, heirlooms and entire generations of memories. Somewhere between one and two million people are believed to have died in the communal violence that followed.   For millions, "home" suddenly became a place they could never return to. While films like Garam Hava, Pinjar, Earth, Train to Pakistan and Manto have explored different aspects of Partition, mainstream Indian cinema has largely focused on political events, wars or national identity. Stories centred on everyday compassion during those violent months remain surprisingly rare. That is the space Batwara 1947 hopes to occupy. What made the original play so extraordinary? Unlike many Partition stories driven by violence, Wajahat's play asks a quieter question: what happens when people choose kindness over hatred? Its characters represent contrasting responses to a fractured society. Mai becomes the emotional heart of the story, refusing to abandon the only home she has ever known.Sikander Mirza's family slowly discovers that compassion often proves stronger than prejudice.The local Maulvi stands as the voice of humanity and religious tolerance.Yaqoob Pehalwan embodies the dangers of fanaticism and communal politics.Refugee poet Nasir Kazmi carries the pain of displacement, reminding audiences that exile leaves wounds invisible to the eye.Instead of simplifying history into heroes and villains, the play presents ordinary individuals navigating extraordinary circumstances. How did a reported ban fail to stop its journey? Ironically, the play's influence only grew with time. Although it reportedly faced restrictions in Pakistan, Jis Lahore Nai Vekhya, O Jamya E Nai travelled far beyond political boundaries. It has been staged not only across India but also in Lahore, Karachi, Dubai, New York and numerous international theatre festivals. One of its widely appreciated productions came in 2018, when Jaipur-based theatre artist Shakur Khan brought the play to the stage. It later featured at NAPA's International Performing Arts Festival in Pakistan through an adaptation by Anwer Jafri and co-direction by Jafri and veteran theatre personality Sheema Kermani. Its enduring popularity suggests that while governments may draw borders, stories often refuse to recognise them. Why is adapting it into a film such a significant moment? Theatre reaches audiences one performance at a time. Cinema reaches generations. By bringing Wajahat's story to the big screen, Batwara 1947 introduces a timeless narrative to viewers who may know Partition only through textbooks or family anecdotes. The film also arrives at a time when conversations around identity, migration and coexistence remain as relevant as ever. Rather than revisiting history to reopen wounds, it appears to ask whether empathy can survive even during humanity's darkest moments. That makes the adaptation more than just another historical drama—it becomes an opportunity to preserve memory. Can films rewrite the way we remember history? Perhaps not. But they can reshape how we feel about it. The greatest Partition stories have never been about maps. They have been about kitchens left unfinished, childhood lanes abandoned overnight, neighbours separated forever and strangers choosing compassion when hatred seemed easier. That is what has kept Jis Lahore Nai Vekhya, O Jamya E Nai alive for nearly four decades. If Batwara 1947 captures even a fraction of that emotional depth, it could become far more than another Independence-week release. It may finally bring to mainstream Indian cinema a chapter of Partition history that has lived for decades on the stage—where loss, memory and humanity mattered more than the line that divided two nations.

title: Theft: ମନ୍ଦିରରୁ ଲୁଟ୍: ସୁନା, ରୁପା ଅଳଙ୍କାର ସହ ୩୦ ହଜାର ଟଙ୍କାର ଦାନ ଅର୍ଥ ଚୋରି
Fire Breaks Out in Srinagar's Khanyar ; Two Residential Houses Damaged
description:
Srinagar, June 27: At around 3:00 a.m the State Fire Control Room, Batamaloo, received a fire call from Tabardar Mohalla, Khanyar. Acting swiftly, fire tenders from Fire Station Babdemb, Fire Station Rainawari, Fire Station M.R. Gunj, followed by Fire & Emergency Services Headquarters, was rushed to the spot.
Considering the highly congested nature of the area, additional fire tenders were dispatched as reinforcement to assist in the firefighting operation.
Upon arrival, firefighters found a four-storeyed residential house in which the 3rd and 4th Story was engulfed in flames, while the attic portion of an adjacent three-storeyed residential house had also caught fire.
Firefighters immediately launched an intensive firefighting operation to prevent the blaze from spreading further.
In the incident, two residential houses sustained damage. The four-storeyed residential house In which the 3rd story and the Attic was completely gutted in the fire and the Rest 2nd Floor and Ground floor got damaged due to water, heat and smoke, while the three-storeyed residential house was partially damaged.
The operation was carried out under the overall supervision of ADGP/Director, Fire & Emergency Services Alok Kumar - IPS while senior officials remained on the ground to monitor and coordinate the operation.
The cause of the fire is yet to be ascertained and is under investigation.

title: Us-sanctions: ଗୃହଯୁଦ୍ଧ ଗ୍ରସ୍ତ ସୁଦାନକୁ ଅସ୍ତ୍ରଶସ୍ତ୍ର-ବିସ୍ଫୋରକ ପଠାଇବା ଅଭିଯୋଗ: ଭାରତୀୟ କମ୍ପାନି ଏବଂ ସିଇଓଙ୍କ ଉପରେ ଆମେରିକାର ପ୍ରତିବନ୍ଧକ ଲାଗୁ
description:
ନୂଆଦିଲ୍ଲୀ/ୱାସିଂଟନ: ଆମେରିକା ପକ୍ଷରୁ ଜଣେ ଭାରତୀୟ ସିଇଓଙ୍କ ସମେତ ଆଠ ଜଣ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତି ଏବଂ ସଂସ୍ଥା ଉପରେ ପ୍ରତିବନ୍ଧକ ଲଗାଯାଇଛି। ସେମାନଙ୍କ ବିରୋଧରେ ଗୃହଯୁଦ୍ଧଗ୍ରସ୍ତ ସୁଦାନକୁ ଅସ୍ତ୍ରଶସ୍ତ୍ର ଏବଂ ବିସ୍ଫୋରକ ପଠାଇବା ତଥା ଗୃହଯୁଦ୍ଧକୁ ବଢ଼ାଇବା ଅଭିଯୋଗ ରହିଛି। ଆମେରିକାର ଟ୍ରେଜେରୀ ବିଭାଗର ବୈଦେଶିକ ସମ୍ପତ୍ତି ନିୟନ୍ତ୍ରଣ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାଳୟ ଏହି ସୂଚନା ଦେଇଛି। ଏହି ପ୍ରତିବନ୍ଧକ ବିଷୟରେ ସୂଚନା ଦେଇ, ଆମେରିକୀୟ ବିଦେଶ ବିଭାଗ କହିଛି ଯେ, ଯେଉଁ ନେଟୱାର୍କଗୁଡ଼ିକ ବିରୋଧରେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟାନୁଷ୍ଠାନ ନିଆଯାଇଛି ସେମାନେ ସୁଦାନ ସଶସ୍ତ୍ର ବାହିନୀ ଏବଂ ରାପିଡ୍ ସପୋର୍ଟ ଫୋର୍ସେସ୍ ଉଭୟ ପକ୍ଷରୁ ସଂଘର୍ଷ ବଢ଼ାଇବାରେ ସହାୟତା କରିଛନ୍ତି। ଆମେରିକୀୟ ବିଦେଶ ବିଭାଗ ମୁଖପାତ୍ର ଟମି ପିଗଟ୍ ଏକ ବିବୃତିରେ କହିଛନ୍ତି, ଏହି ନେଟୱାର୍କଗୁଡ଼ିକ ସୁଦାନ ସଶସ୍ତ୍ର ବାହିନୀ ଏବଂ ରାପିଡ୍ ସପୋର୍ଟ ଫୋର୍ସେସ୍ ଉଭୟକୁ ଅସ୍ତ୍ରଶସ୍ତ୍ର, ବିସ୍ଫୋରକ ଏବଂ ଲଢ଼ିବା ପାଇଁ ବିଦେଶୀରୁ ସଦସ୍ୟ ଯୋଗାଇଥିବା ଅଭିଯୋଗ ହୋଇଛି। ସେମାନଙ୍କ ଯୋଗୁଁ ବିଶ୍ୱରେ ମାନବିକ ସଙ୍କଟ ସୃଷ୍ଟି ହୋଇଛି ତଥା ଆତଙ୍କବାଦୀ ଗୋଷ୍ଠୀଗୁଡ଼ିକୁ ଆକ୍ରମଣ କରିବାର ସୁଯୋଗ ଦେଇଛି।
ଏକ କମ୍ପାନିକୁ ୨୦୦ ରୁ ଅଧିକ ବିସ୍ଫୋରକ ଏବଂ ସମ୍ପର୍କିତ ସାମଗ୍ରୀ ଯୋଗାଇଥିଲା
ଆମେରିକା ଦ୍ୱାରା ନିଷିଦ୍ଧ କରାଯାଇଥିବା ସଂସ୍ଥାମାନଙ୍କ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଭାରତୀୟ ଫାର୍ମ ଏସବିଏଲ ଏନର୍ଜି ଲିମିଟେଡର ସିଇଓ ଆଲୋକ ଚୌଧୁରୀ ମଧ୍ୟ ଅଛନ୍ତି। ଏସବିଏଲ ଏନର୍ଜି ଆମେମ୍ ଏକ୍ସପ୍ଲୋସିଭ୍ସ ପ୍ରାଇଭେଟ୍ ଲିମିଟେଡ୍ ଭାବରେ ମଧ୍ୟ ଜଣାଶୁଣା। ଆମେରିକା ଦାବି କରିଛି ଯେ ଏସବିଏଲ ଏନର୍ଜି ସୁଦାନର ସଶସ୍ତ୍ର ବାହିନୀ ପାଇଁ ଅସ୍ତ୍ରଶସ୍ତ୍ର ଭଣ୍ଡାର ରକ୍ଷଣାବେକ୍ଷଣ କରୁଥିବା ଏକ କମ୍ପାନିକୁ ୨୦୦ ରୁ ଅଧିକ ବିସ୍ଫୋରକଏବଂ ସମ୍ପର୍କିତ ସାମଗ୍ରୀ ଯୋଗାଇଥିଲା।ଆମେରିକାର ଟ୍ରେଜେରୀ ବିଭାଗ ଏସବିଏଲ ଏନର୍ଜି ଲିମିଟେଡ୍ ସହିତ ସୁଦାନ ଏବଂ ଇଜିପ୍ଟରେ ଥିବା ଅନ୍ୟ କମ୍ପାନିଗୁଡ଼ିକ ଉପରେ ପ୍ରତିବନ୍ଧକ ଲଗାଇଛି। ଆମେରିକାର ଟ୍ରେଜେରୀ ବିଭାଗ ଅନୁଯାୟୀ, ଏସବିଏଲ ଏନର୍ଜି ସୁଦାନର ଟାର୍ଗେଟ ମଲ୍ଟି ଆକ୍ଟିଭିଟିଜ୍ କମ୍ପାନି(ଟିଏମଏସି)କୁ ବିସ୍ଫୋରକ ଏବଂ ସମ୍ପର୍କିତ ସାମଗ୍ରୀ ଯୋଗାଇଥିଲା, ଯାହା ସୁଦାନର ସଶସ୍ତ୍ର ବାହିନୀ ଦ୍ୱାରା ନିର୍ମିତ ବୋମାରେ ବ୍ୟବହୃତ ହେଉଥିଲା। ଟିଏମଏସି ଏବଂ ଏହାର ଜେନେରାଲ ମ୍ୟାନେଜର ତାରିକ ହୁସେନ ମହମ୍ମଦ ମଦନିଙ୍କୁ ମଧ୍ୟ କଳା ତାଲିକାଭୁକ୍ତ କରାଯାଇଛି। ମଦନି ସୁଦାନର ସର୍ବବୃହତ ପ୍ରତିରକ୍ଷା କମ୍ପାନି ଡିଫେନ୍ସ ଇଣ୍ଡଷ୍ଟ୍ରିଜ୍ ସିଷ୍ଟମ୍ସ (ଡିଆଇଏସ)ର ଜଣେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟନିର୍ବାହୀ। ଡିଆଇଏସ ସୁଦାନର ସଶସ୍ତ୍ର ବାହିନୀର ଗୋଳାବାରୁଦ, ଯାନବାହାନ ଏବଂ ଅନ୍ୟାନ୍ୟ ସାମଗ୍ରୀର ଭଣ୍ଡାର ରକ୍ଷଣାବେକ୍ଷଣ କରିଥାଏ। ଏହି ଅସ୍ତ୍ରଶସ୍ତ୍ର ଏବଂ ସାମଗ୍ରୀ ପ୍ରାୟତଃ ଇରାନ ଏବଂ ଅନ୍ୟାନ୍ୟ ବାହ୍ୟ ସହଯୋଗୀଙ୍କଠାରୁ ପ୍ରାପ୍ତ ହୋଇଥାଏ।
Cannibalism Case: ହଙ୍ଗେରୀରେ ନରଭକ୍ଷୀ ମାମଲା: ହସ୍ପିଟାଲ କର୍ମଚାରୀଙ୍କ ଉପରେ ମୃତ ମାନବ ଅଙ୍ଗ ଖାଇବା ଓ ଶ୍ମଶାନରୁ ମୃତଦେହ ଚୋରି କରିବାର ଅଭିଯୋଗ

title: Gold rate today- 24k vs 22k vs 20k vs 18k
Raj Thackeray: यांच्या वर्दीचा खाकी रंग पोलीस दलाचा आहे असा आमचा समज होता…; राज ठाकरेंचा आयपीएस विश्वास नांगरे पाटलांवर निशाणा
description: मुंबई | Mumbaiआयपीएस अधिकारी विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांनी नुकत्याच केलेल्या एका वक्तव्यावरून नव्या राजकीय वादाला तोंड फुटलं. एका सरकारी नोकरीत असलेल्या, त्यातही अत्यंत जबाबदार पोलीस अधिकाऱ्याने अशा कार्यक्रमात जाऊन एका विशिष्ट संघटनेबद्दल जाहीरपणे निष्ठा दाखवणे कितपत योग्य आहे, असा रोखठोक सवाल राज ठाकरे यांनी उपस्थित केला. नुकतीच नागपूर पोलीस आयुक्त म्हणून बदली झालेल्या विश्वास नांगरे […]
content:
मुंबई | Mumbaiआयपीएस अधिकारी विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांनी नुकत्याच केलेल्या एका वक्तव्यावरून नव्या राजकीय वादाला तोंड फुटलं. एका सरकारी नोकरीत असलेल्या, त्यातही अत्यंत जबाबदार पोलीस अधिकाऱ्याने अशा कार्यक्रमात जाऊन एका विशिष्ट संघटनेबद्दल जाहीरपणे निष्ठा दाखवणे कितपत योग्य आहे, असा रोखठोक सवाल राज ठाकरे यांनी उपस्थित केला.



नुकतीच नागपूर पोलीस आयुक्त म्हणून बदली झालेल्या विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांनी राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघाच्या कार्यक्रमामध्ये सहभाग घेतला व राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघाची स्तुती केली. या कार्यक्रमातील विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांचे वक्तव्य व्हायरल होत आहे. विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांनी राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघाची स्तुती केल्यानंतर विविध प्रतिक्रिया उमटत आहेत. याचदरम्यान, मनसेप्रमुख राज ठाकरे यांनी सोशल मीडियावर पोस्ट करत विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांच्यावर सवाल उपस्थित केला आहे.



विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांच्याबद्दल राज ठाकरे काय म्हणाले?आयपीएस अधिकारी विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांना अचानक दुहेरी निष्ठा दाखवण्याची इच्छा झाली आहे असं दिसतंय. ते एक पोलीस अधिकारी आहेत, त्यामुळे त्यांची निष्ठा फक्त आणि फक्त पोलीस दलाची जी कर्तव्य आहेत त्याच्याशी असायला हवी. पण इथे नांगरे पाटील यांना त्यांची राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघाशी असलेली निष्ठा दाखवावीशी वाटत आहे. सध्या राज्यात हिंदू संमेलनं भरवली जात आहेत आणि त्यातल्या एका संमेलनात जाऊन विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांनी राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघ, त्यांचे संस्थापक यांच्यावर स्तुतिसुमने उधळली. मुळात ही संमेलनं भरवली जात आहेत ती जरी अराजकीय आहेत असं दाखवलं जात असली तरी ती राजकीयच आहेत. आणि ती भरवणारे कोण आहेत आणि त्यांचे हेतू नक्की काय आहेत, याचा थोडा शोध घेतला किंवा विचार जरी केला तरी या मागचे हात कुठे आहेत हे कळेल. अर्थात प्रत्येक राजकीय पक्षाला किंवा अराजकीय संघटना म्हणवून घेणाऱ्यांनी काय कार्यक्रम घ्यावेत आणि काय त्यातून सांगावे हा त्यांचा, प्रत्येक पक्षाचा आणि संघटनेचा विषय आहे , त्यामुळे त्याबद्दल बोलायचा किंवा त्यावर भाष्य करण्याचा प्रश्नच उद्भवत नाही. विषय एका सरकारी नोकरीवर असणाऱ्याने, त्यातही पोलिस अधिकाऱ्याने तेथे जावं का हा प्रश्न आहे, असं राज ठाकरे म्हणाले.




आयपीएस अधिकारी श्री. विश्वास नांगरे पाटील यांना अचानक दुहेरी निष्ठा दाखवण्याची इच्छा झाली आहे असं दिसतंय. ते एक पोलीस अधिकारी आहेत, त्यामुळे त्यांची निष्ठा फक्त आणि फक्त पोलीस दलाची जी कर्तव्य आहेत त्याच्याशी असायला हवी. पण इथे नांगरे पाटील यांना त्यांची राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक…— Raj Thackeray (@RajThackeray) June 27, 2026




नांगरे पाटील अशा ठिकाणी जाऊन राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघाची तोंडभरून स्तुती करत आहेत. आपण पोलीस अधिकारी आहोत, निष्पक्षता ही अधिकाऱ्याकडून अपेक्षित असते याचं भान पण तुमचं गेलं? तुम्हाला संघाबद्दल आपुलकी असेल, तर ती मनात ठेवा किंवा जाहीरपणे बोलायचं असेल तर सेवेचा राजीनामा द्या आणि संघात किंवा भाजपमध्ये सामील व्हा. सध्या तसंही गेली अनेक वर्ष मर्जीतल्या अधिकाऱ्यांसाठी पुनर्वसन हमी योजना सुरु आहेच. तुमचंही पुनर्वसन होईल. नांगरे पाटील यांच्या वर्दीचा जो खाकी रंग आहे तो पोलीस दलाचा आहे असा आमच्या सगळ्यांचा समज होता, तो रंग संघाच्या आधीच्या खाकीचा आहे हे आत्ताच कळलं. नांगरे पाटील यांचं सोडा, ते भरकटले असतील. पण अशी दुहेरी निष्ठा मुख्यमंत्र्यांना आणि गृहमंत्र्यांना चालते? उद्या तुम्ही सत्तेवरून गेल्यानंतर दुसऱ्या एखाद्या संघटनेच्या कार्यक्रमाला मग एखादा अधिकारी गेला तर तेव्हा बोंब मारू नका…एखाद्या अधिकाऱ्याच्या कृतीचं समर्थन (अजून केलेलं नाही तरी पण गप्प बसणं हेही समर्थनच झालं) करताना आपण काय चूकीचे पायंडे पाडत आहोत याचं भान कोणताही सत्ताधारी विसरतो तेव्हा आपण भविष्यातल्या येणाऱ्या पिढ्यांसमोर काय वाढून ठेवणार आहोत हेही विसरतोय?, असा सवाल राज ठाकरेंनी उपस्थित केला.



NDCC Bank News: जिल्हा बँक पुनरुज्जीवनाचा मार्ग मोकळा, ‘इतक्या’ कोटींच्या निधीची पुरवणी मागण्यांत तरतूद



2012 ला रझा अकादमीच्या विरोधात महाराष्ट्र नवनिर्माण सेनेने काढलेल्या मोर्च्यात एका कॉन्स्टेबलने येऊन, पोलिसांच्या पाठीशी उभे राहिलात म्हणून माझं अभिनंदन केलं होतं. तर त्यावर तत्कालीन सरकारने निष्पक्षता सोडली म्हणून त्यांना सक्तीच्या रजेवर पाठवलं होतं. मग हीच भूमिका सरकार नांगरे पाटील यांच्या बाबत घेणार का ? का ही दुहेरी निष्ठा सरकारला चालते किंवा ती दाखवयालाच हवी असं वाटतं?, आमदार, खासदार यांना ‘आमच्याकडे या’ म्हणलं की पळत जावं लागतं, अशी परिस्थिती आणून ठेवली आहे. सध्या विरोधी आमदार, खासदार उरलेत का अशी शंका वाटत असताना जर सरकारी अधिकारी पण जर निष्पक्ष नाही राहिले तर लोकांनी बघायचं कोणाकडे? नांगरे पाटील असोत किंवा अजून कोणी असोत यांना विनंती आहे की सरकारला तुम्ही त्यांच्या किंवा त्यांच्या मागच्या संघटनांच्या मागे धावलात तर त्यांना हे हवंच आहे. परंतू नांगरे पाटील तुम्ही एक चांगले कार्यक्षम अधिकारी आहात, तुम्ही तरी तुमची सद्सद्विवेक राजकीय पक्ष आणि संघटनांसमोर गहाण टाकू नका, असं आवाहनही देखील राज ठाकरेंनी यावेळी केलं.

title: କାଜୁ ଜଙ୍ଗଲରେ ଝୁଲିପଡ଼ିଲେ ସଞ୍ଜୟ: ବଢୁଛି ସନ୍ଦେହ
description:
ମହାକାଳପଡ଼ା,୨୭ା୬ (ପ୍ରତାପ ଚନ୍ଦ୍ର ସ୍ବାଇଁ):କେନ୍ଦ୍ରାପଡ଼ା ଜିଲା ମହାକାଳପଡ଼ା ବ୍ଲକ ଜମ୍ବୁ ସାମୁଦ୍ରିକ ଥାନା ଅନ୍ତର୍ଗତ ରାମନଗର ପଞ୍ଚାୟତ ପିତାପାଟ ଚୌଧୁରୀ କାଜୁ ଜଙ୍ଗଲରେ ଜଣେ ଯୁବକ ଗୁରୁବାର ବିଳମ୍ବିତ ରାତିରେ ଫାଶୀ ଲଗାଇ ଆତ୍ମହତ୍ୟା କରିଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ହେଲେ ସଞ୍ଜୟ ମଣ୍ଡଳ(୨୬)। ବାପା ଗୌର ମଣ୍ଡଳ ଥାନାରେ ଲିଖିତ ଅଭିଯୋଗ କରିବା ପରେ ଏକ ଅପମୃତ୍ୟୁ ମାମଲା (ନଂ. ୮/୨୪ ) ରୁଜୁ କରି ପୋଲିସ ତଦନ୍ତ ଚଳାଇଛି। ପୋଲିସ ମୃତଦେହ ଜବତକରି ବ୍ୟବଚ୍ଛେଦ ପାଇଁ କେନ୍ଦ୍ରାପଡ଼ା […]
The post କାଜୁ ଜଙ୍ଗଲରେ ଝୁଲିପଡ଼ିଲେ ସଞ୍ଜୟ: ବଢୁଛି ସନ୍ଦେହ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: PM Modi leaves for Seychelles; to attend golden jubilee of country's National Day
description:
New Delhi, June 27: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday left for Seychelles on a three-day visit during which he will attend the golden jubilee celebrations of the National Day of that country and have bilateral discussions with President Patrick Herminie to further strengthen the friendship.
In his departure statement, the prime minister said Seychelles is a valued maritime neighbour and a key partner in the 'Vision MAHASAGAR' and India's shared commitment to the Global South.
Modi also said that he looks forward to the bilateral discussions aimed at further strengthening the enduring friendship.
"Together, we will work to advance the progress of our peoples, and promote security and prosperity in the Indian Ocean region," he said.
The prime minister said he is undertaking the state visit from June 27-29 at the invitation of President of Seychelles Herminie, during which he will participate in the golden jubilee celebrations of the National Day of Seychelles as the Guest of Honour.
"Leaving for Seychelles, where I will take part in their National Day celebrations. This year, it is even more special because it is the Golden Jubilee celebrations," he said in a post on 'X'.
In the statement, Modi said this year also marks the 50th anniversary of the establishment of India-Seychelles diplomatic relations, which are "rooted in mutual trust, shared democratic values, respect for diversity and deep affinity between our peoples".
"Building on the successful State visit of President Herminie to India in February 2026, I look forward to our discussions aimed at further strengthening our enduring friendship. Together, we will work to advance the progress of our peoples, and promote security and prosperity in the Indian Ocean region," he said.
During the visit, Modi will have the honour of becoming the first Indian prime minister to address the National Assembly of Seychelles. He said this historic opportunity reflects the strong democratic values and parliamentary traditions that bind the two nations.
Modi said he also looks forward to interacting with the vibrant Indian community in Seychelles, who have been nurturing the special friendship between India and Seychelles for generations, and serving as a living bridge between the two nations.
"I am confident that my visit will further deepen the longstanding bonds between the two countries, enhance maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean region, and advance our shared vision of a secure, peaceful and prosperous Indian Ocean region," he said.
Officials said a contingent of the Indian armed forces, along with two ships of the Indian Navy, will participate in the celebrations.
Modi last visited Seychelles in 2015. India and Seychelles share a longstanding partnership rooted in shared historical, cultural and people-to-people ties.
As a key maritime neighbour in the Indian Ocean region, Seychelles holds a special place in India's Vision MAHASAGAR (Mutual and Holistic Advancement for Security and Growth Across Regions) and our commitment to the Global South, the officials said.

title: The Prodigy XI: Will Vaibhav Sooryavanshi make it?
description: History’s verdict on teenage prodigies is clear-eyed and unsentimental. Across nearly 150 years of international cricket, it has produced a Tendulkar and a Sobers, a Waqar and a Mushtaq. The queue to join them is long, the attrition rate high, and it mostly ends in footnotes.

title: বিয়া ঠিক হৈছিল যদিও কেতনৰ তপা মূৰক লৈ শ্ৰেয়াৰ আছিল অসন্তুষ্টি...
Inside Carry On Jatta actor Sargun Mehta and Ramayana's Lakshman Ravi Dubey's Rs 150 crore empire: Rs 11 lakh-a-month sea-facing Bandra home, BMW, Jaguar, Dreamiyata business and more
description: Ahead of Ramayana, explore Ravi Dubey and Sargun Mehta's lavish lifestyle, including their estimated Rs 150 crore net worth, Rs 11 lakh-a-month sea-facing Bandra home, BMW and Jaguar collection, Dreamiyata Entertainment, successful careers and growing business empire.
content: Ravi Dubey is gearing up for one of the biggest milestones of his career as he prepares to play Lakshman in Nitesh Tiwari's much-awaited Ramayana, headlined by Ranbir Kapoor. Ever since the film's first glimpse was unveiled, fans have been eager to see the actor in his new avatar, with wife Sargun Mehta proudly cheering him on. At the same time, Sargun, best known for films such as Carry On Jatta, Saunkan Saunkne, Qismat 2 and Moh, continues to dominate the Punjabi film industry as one of its biggest stars. While the couple are winning hearts with their on-screen achievements, they are equally making headlines for the life they have built together off screen. From successful acting careers and a booming production house to a luxurious sea-facing Mumbai home, premium cars and a business empire worth over Rs 150 crore, Ravi and Sargun have quietly transformed themselves from television favourites into one of the entertainment industry's most successful power couples.How did Ravi Dubey build his acting career? Ravi Dubey began his television journey with Stree Teri Kahani before steadily becoming one of the most recognisable faces on Indian television. Over the years, he delivered memorable performances in popular shows including Doli Saja Ke, 12/24 Karol Bagh, Saas Bina Sasural, Parvarrish – Kuchh Khattee Kuchh Meethi and Jamai Raja. His career later expanded into the digital space with projects such as Jamai 2.0 and Matsya Kaand, while hosting reality shows further strengthened his popularity. His biggest professional leap now comes with Ramayana, where he essays the role of Lakshman in what is being billed as one of Indian cinema's most ambitious productions.     Why is Sargun Mehta one of Punjabi cinema's biggest stars? Sargun Mehta has transformed herself from a television actress into one of the highest-paid and most successful stars in Punjabi cinema. She first won hearts through television shows such as 12/24 Karol Bagh, Apno Ke Liye Geeta Ka Dharmayudh and Phulwa, before shifting her focus to films. Today, her filmography includes commercially successful titles like Angrej, Saunkan Saunkne, Qismat 2 and Moh. She has also appeared in Hindi projects and popular music videos including Titliaan, Taare Baliye and Ve Haaniyaan, expanding her fan base across the country. Did you know? Sargun reportedly commands around Rs 2 crore per Punjabi film, making her one of the industry's top earners. What makes Dreamiyata Entertainment a success story? Acting may have introduced Ravi and Sargun to fame, but entrepreneurship has significantly expanded their wealth. In 2019, the couple launched Dreamiyata Entertainment Pvt Ltd, which has gone on to produce several successful television dramas and Punjabi films. Some of the banner's notable projects include: UdaariyaanJunooniyattDaalchiniPunjabi films including Saunkan Saunkne and MohMultiple web series and music videosThe couple also expanded their digital ambitions by launching Dreamiyata Drama in November 2024, adding another vertical to their growing entertainment business.                 Reports also suggest that Sargun holds around 55 per cent ownership in the company, making her the majority stakeholder.            View this post on Instagram            A post shared by Sarggun mehta (@sargunmehta) Where do Ravi Dubey and Sargun Mehta live now? The couple recently made headlines after leasing an ultra-luxurious sea-facing apartment in Mumbai's upscale Pali Hill locality in Bandra West. Industry reports claim the residence was previously occupied by Bollywood superstar Aamir Khan. Located inside Bella Vista building, the apartment overlooks the Arabian Sea and reportedly comes with a monthly rent of Rs 11 lakh. The five-year lease was secured with an estimated security deposit of Rs 80 lakh, Magicbricks reported. The agreement also includes a yearly escalation clause, taking the monthly rent to nearly Rs 13.37 lakh by the final year. The move places Ravi and Sargun among several Bollywood celebrities who call Bandra's elite neighbourhood home.            View this post on Instagram            A post shared by Ravie Dubey (@ravidubey2312) What does their home look like from the inside? Long before shifting to their new Bandra address, Ravi and Sargun's home had become a favourite among fans thanks to glimpses shared on social media. Their interiors beautifully combine modern luxury with Japanese-inspired minimalism, creating a sophisticated yet warm atmosphere. Some standout features include: A dedicated photo wall celebrating their relationship and journey together.Elegant white and gold interiors that make the home feel bright and spacious.A customised entertainment bar designed for family and friends.A luxurious bathroom featuring an eye-catching chandelier.A dedicated makeup room created especially for Sargun.Functional yet stylish furniture that reflects Ravi's preference for uncluttered spaces.            View this post on Instagram            A post shared by Sarggun mehta (@sargunmehta)Which luxury cars are parked in Ravi and Sargun's garage? The couple's love for premium automobiles is evident from their impressive collection. Their garage houses several luxury vehicles that perfectly complement their lifestyle. Among the cars they reportedly own are the BMW X5, Jaguar XJ and Audi A8L. Over the years, Ravi and Sargun have also gifted each other luxury cars to celebrate professional achievements and wedding anniversaries, making their collection even more special. How much is Ravi Dubey and Sargun Mehta's net worth? The couple's income now comes from multiple sources rather than acting alone. Apart from television, films and web series, they earn through brand endorsements, production ventures, digital content and hosting assignments. According to Magicbricks and Indiatimes, Ravi Dubey's individual net worth is estimated between Rs 60 crore and Rs 70 crore. Sargun Mehta's wealth at approximately Rs 84 crore. Together, their combined fortune is estimated to be over Rs 150 crore. Some reports estimate their annual earnings at over Rs 150 crore, with Ravi contributing around Rs 67 crore and Sargun approximately Rs 84 crore through acting, production, endorsements and other business ventures. While these figures vary across reports, there is little doubt that the couple have built one of the most successful entertainment businesses to emerge from Indian television. When did Ravi Dubey and Sargun Mehta get married? Their love story began during their television days and gradually blossomed into one of the industry's most admired relationships. After dating for several years, Ravi Dubey and Sargun Mehta tied the knot in 2013. More than a decade later, they continue to support each other's ambitions, whether it is Ravi stepping into mythology with Ramayana or Sargun dominating Punjabi cinema. Together, they have shown that sustained success comes not only from talent but also from smart business decisions, making them one of the entertainment industry's most inspiring power couples.

title: Ketan Agrawal Case: How A Cricket-Field Friendship Led To A Murder Plot
description: Ketan agarwal mursee case: The accused fiancée and her alleged lover spoke for the equivalent of nearly ten full days in the months a wedding was being planned, Times Now’s investigation finds. It began, police say, on a cricket field.
content: They exchanged 2,004 phone calls. They logged close to 238 hours on the line — the better part of ten full days. And through the months that the Agrawal and Goyal families were planning a wedding, police sources told Times Now, Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary were rarely more than a call apart. That call detail record is now central to the Lonavala Rural Police investigation into the death of Ketan Agrawal, a real estate developer from a prominent Pune business family, who died on June 18, 2026, at Lohagad Fort near Lonavala. The death was initially reported as an accidental fall and logged as an Accidental Death Report. Investigators later arrested his fiancee, Siya Goyal, and her alleged boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, on charges of murder and criminal conspiracy.A friendship That Started At The Boundary LineSiya and Chetan first met during local cricket matches, according to police sources. Siya’s brother, Sahil, and Chetan played together; Siya, who often went along to watch, came to know Chetan there. The two reportedly met again at a friend’s Diwali party and began meeting more frequently after that. What started as an acquaintance at the ground, police say, turned into a relationship.The 2,004 calls and 238 hours of conversation, investigators believe, map that relationship across the same window in which both families were moving toward marriage.Siya’s Brother Questioned For 10 HoursOn Friday, police questioned Sahil Goyal, Siya’s brother and Chetan’s old cricket teammate, for nearly ten hours. Officers sought detailed information from him on multiple aspects of the case, sources said. Police are also likely to record the statements of Siya’s parents in the coming days.The man who introduced the two families questioned The Lonavala Rural Police on Friday questioned Narendra Mittal, the maternal uncle of Ketan’s father, Vishal Agrawal. Mittal is the thread connecting the two families: his sister-in-law’s daughter is Siya Goyal. According to Mittal, discussions began in January 2026, and a meeting between the Agrawal and Goyal families was arranged in February 2026 to discuss the marriage. Ketan and his family came to meet Siya, both sides agreed, and the engagement was held at a hotel in Pune.Mittal told police his only role was introducing the two families. He said he had known Siya since childhood, that both she and Ketan appeared well-mannered and respectful, and that he had no knowledge of any relationship between Siya and Chetan. He said the family never imagined Siya could be connected to such an incident.Taken Back To The Lohagad fortAlso on Friday, the police quietly brought both accused to the base of Lohagad Fort at around 11 a.m. as part of the investigation. Investigators asked Siya and Chetan to retrace the exact route each had taken to the fort. There are two approaches to Lohagad, and the two were asked to identify which they had used. After the route reconstruction, both were taken back to the police station.What police allegePolice have laid out a sequence stretching over weeks. On May 31, Siya Goyal allegedly took Agrawal to Lohagad and identified a spot near the Vinchu Kata ridge; a June 4 trip did not happen for some reason. They next went there on June 14, when she allegedly tried to push him off the cliff, but he survived by grabbing a bush and she convinced him it had been an accident. Four days later, on June 18, she allegedly returned with him and, and with Chetan Chaudhary’s assistance, pushed him into a roughly 350-foot gorge. In custody, the two have reportedly blamed each other. Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis has ordered a fast-track trial and the appointment of Ujjwal Nikam as special prosecutor, while Vishal Agrawal has met the CM and is seeking the death penalty for the accused.Both accused remain under investigation. The charges have not been tested in court, and the accused are entitled to the presumption of innocence.

title: ভুৱা চিকিৎসকৰ বিৰুদ্ধে আৰক্ষীৰ অভিযানঃ আটক দুজন চিকিৎসক
Names of six military personnel who died in action during Op Sindoor inscribed at National War Memorial
description:
New Delhi, JunE 27: The names of six military personnel -- five from the Indian Army and one from IAF -- who died in the line of duty during Operation Sindoor last year, have been inscribed on a wall at the National War Memorial, as a tribute to the fallen heroes.
This is the first time since the military operation conducted by the Indian armed forces in May 2025 that the government has released the names of these six personnel.
Operation Sindoor was conducted to avenge the deadly Pahalgam terror attack on April 22 last year.
The names of the six personnel, inscribed on wall no. '3D' of Tyag Chakra at the National War Memorial are -- Subedar Major Pawan Kumar, Rifleman Sunil Kumar (VrC), Lance Naik Dinesh Kumar, Agniveer Mood Muralinaik, Havildar Sunil Kumar Singh from the Army, and Indian Air Force's Sergeant Surendra Kumar.
The unit names of these bravehearts have also been inscribed.
Sources said these six names were inscribed recently on the wall.
These names have also been added to the Roll of Honour on the website of the National War Memorial.
The iconic Memorial stands as a solemn tribute to Indian soldiers who have fallen in the line of duty, defending the nation during wars since Independence.
The centrepiece of the memorial, located at the India Gate complex, is an obelisk along with the eternal flame -- the Amar Jawan Jyoti -- surrounded by concentric circles carrying curved walls, named serially, on which names of the fallen heroes have been inscribed.
On the corner of wall no. '3D', a brick on the top row bears the inscription 'OP SINDOOR', the one below it '2025', followed by names of the six personnel in a column, on different rows of bricks.
India launched Operation Sindoor early on May 7 last year and decimated multiple terror infrastructure in Pakistan and Pakistan-Occupied-Kashmir (PoK).
Pakistan also launched offensives against India, and all subsequent counter-offensives by India were also carried out under Operation Sindoor.
The military conflict between the two nuclear-armed neighbours halted after they reached an understanding on the evening of May 10.

title: Criminal charges against Gautam Adani: US court orders Justice Department to justify decision to drop fraud charges
description: Brooklyn-based U.S. District Judge Nicholas Garaufis stated that federal prosecutors' May 18 announcement to abandon the case, which accused Adani of securities fraud and wire fraud linked to an alleged bribery plot, failed to provide a sufficient explanation for the sudden reversal.
content: A U.S. judge ordered the U.S. Department of Justice on Friday to explain its reasoning for dropping criminal charges against billionaire Gautam Adani, declining an immediate ruling to dismiss the case as requested by Adani’s legal team, reported news agency Reuters. Brooklyn-based U.S. District Judge Nicholas Garaufis stated that federal prosecutors' May 18 announcement to abandon the case, which accused Adani of securities fraud and wire fraud linked to an alleged bribery plot, failed to provide a sufficient explanation for the sudden reversal."The Government's terse, bland and conclusory statement affords the court neither a sufficient basis to reach any conclusion, nor the opportunity to conduct any analysis of the Government's request for dismissal," Garaufis wrote, giving the Justice Department a deadline of July 13 to provide additional details.    Related News | Relief for Gautam Adani in US criminal case soon? Billionaire moves court to end charges - DetailsThe Adani case was brought in 2024 at the end of Democratic President Joe Biden's term. The decision to drop the charges marked the latest instance in which the Justice Department has sought to end a high-profile white collar criminal prosecution during Republican President Donald Trump's second term in the White House.Legal experts say U.S. judges have little discretion to compel prosecutors to continue with criminal cases they no longer wish to pursue, but the charges remain officially pending until Garaufis orders them dismissed.Initially filed in 2024 at the end of Democratic President Joe Biden's administration, the move to drop the Adani case marks the latest high-profile white-collar prosecution the Justice Department has moved to terminate under Republican President Donald Trump's second term in the White House.While legal experts note that U.S. judges have little discretion to force prosecutors to advance criminal cases they choose to abandon, the charges will remain officially active until Garaufis signs off on the dismissal.A representative for the Brooklyn U.S. Attorney's office, which brought the charges, chose not to comment on the matter, Reuters reported.Gautam Adani-led Adani Group has maintained its innocence throughout the proceedings, and the billionaire himself has not travelled to the U.S. to appear in court regarding the allegations.Robert Giuffra, Adani’s lawyer, pointed to a letter sent to Judge Garaufis on Wednesday, arguing that the case should be dismissed because it exceeds the jurisdiction of U.S. law and because prosecutors cannot substantiate the bribery allegations in India.Defense cites 'legal and factual weaknesses'Adani was charged in 2024 for conspiring to bribe Indian government officials to secure solar energy plant approvals for an Adani Group subsidiary, while misleading American investors with false assurances regarding the conglomerate's anti-corruption policies.Parallel, civil charges were also pursued by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). The SEC has since finalised a settlement requiring Adani to pay $6 million, while his nephew, Sagar Adani, will pay $12 million.Separately, Adani Enterprises Limited (ADEL.NS) has agreed to a $275 million settlement with the U.S. Department of the Treasury over alleged breaches of sanctions against Iran.In his June 24 letter to Garaufis, Giuffra said lawyers for Adani and his co-defendants had several meetings with Justice Department officials and ⁠submitted nearly 500 pages of materials to convince them the case was flawed."The DOJ’s decision reflects its careful consideration of the indictment’s legal and factual weaknesses," Giuffra wrote.'Prosecutorial discretion' citedIn their brief letter to Garaufis last month, senior DOJ officials noted that the agency had determined, "in its prosecutorial discretion, not to devote further resources to these criminal charges against individual defendants."Notably, the rank-and-file prosecutors who originally built and filed the case did not sign the dismissal request.Last year, Washington-based Justice Department officials dropped Biden-era corruption charges against former New York Mayor Eric Adams. over the objections of the career prosecutors who brought the case, several of whom resigned.

title: NDCC Bank News: जिल्हा बँक पुनरुज्जीवनाचा मार्ग मोकळा, ‘इतक्या’ कोटींच्या निधीची पुरवणी मागण्यांत तरतूद
description: नाशिक | प्रतिनिधीआर्थिक अडचणीत सापडलेल्या नाशिक जिल्हा मध्यवर्ती सहकारी बँकेला (एनडीसीसी) अखेर मोठा दिलासा मिळाला आहे. बँकेला संजीवनी देणाऱ्या ६७२ कोटी रुपयांच्या मदतीचा मार्ग आता पूर्णपणे खुला झाला आहे. राज्य शासनाने पावसाळी अधिवेशनात सादर केलेल्या पुरवणी मागण्यांमध्ये या निधीची अधिकृत तरतूद केल्याने जिल्हा बँकेला प्रत्यक्षात ही मदत मिळण्याचा मार्ग सुकर झाला आहे. भारतीय रिझर्व्ह बँकेच्या […]
content:
नाशिक | प्रतिनिधीआर्थिक अडचणीत सापडलेल्या नाशिक जिल्हा मध्यवर्ती सहकारी बँकेला (एनडीसीसी) अखेर मोठा दिलासा मिळाला आहे. बँकेला संजीवनी देणाऱ्या ६७२ कोटी रुपयांच्या मदतीचा मार्ग आता पूर्णपणे खुला झाला आहे. राज्य शासनाने पावसाळी अधिवेशनात सादर केलेल्या पुरवणी मागण्यांमध्ये या निधीची अधिकृत तरतूद केल्याने जिल्हा बँकेला प्रत्यक्षात ही मदत मिळण्याचा मार्ग सुकर झाला आहे. भारतीय रिझर्व्ह बँकेच्या (आरबीआय) नियमांनुसार, सर्व जिल्हा मध्यवर्ती बँकांना ९ टक्के भांडवल पर्याप्तता प्रमाण राखणे बंधनकारक असते. हे प्रमाण विहित मर्यादेपेक्षा कमी झाल्यास संबंधित बँकेवर कायदेशीर कारवाई किंवा परवाना रद्द होण्याचा धोका असतो.



नाशिक, नागपूर आणि धाराशिव या जिल्हा बँका आरबीआयच्या या निकषांची पूर्तता करू शकत नसल्याचे समोर आले होते. या पार्श्वभूमीवर रिझर्व्ह बँकेने राज्य सरकारला या बँकांचे पुनरुज्जीवन व पुनर्मांडवलीकरण करण्याची विनंती केली होती. अन्यथा कारवाईचा इशारा दिला होता. रखडलेला निधी अखेर मंजूर शेतकऱ्यांना पीक कर्जपुरवठा करण्यात जिल्हा बँकांची भूमिका अत्यंत महत्त्वाची असल्याने राज्य सरकारने या बँकांना मदत करण्याचा निर्णय घेतला होता. त्यानुसार राज्य मंत्रिमंडळाच्या बैठकीत मंजुरी देऊन शासन आदेश काढण्यात आला होता. या योजनेअंतर्गत विविध बँकांना खालीलप्रमाणे मदत जाहीर झाली होती.



जिल्हा बँक मंजूर मदत निधीनाशिक जिल्हा बँक ६७२ कोटीबुलडाणा जिल्हा बँक ३०० कोटीनागपूर जिल्हा बँक ८१ कोटीधाराशिव जिल्हा बँक ७४ कोटी



केंद्रीय मंत्रिमंडळ विस्तारात शिंदे गटाच्या ‘या’ खासदारांना लागू शकते लॉटरी, कॅबिनेटसह राज्यमंत्रीपद मिळू शकते



बँक ‘अंधातरी’तून बाहेरवास्तविक पाहता या निधीची तरतूद अर्थसंकल्पीय अधिवेशनात होणे अपेक्षित होते. मात्र, त्यावेळी तरतूद न झाल्याने नाशिक जिल्हा बँकेचे भवितव्य अंधातरी लटकले होते. अखेर चालू पावसाळी अधिवेशनात शासनाने पुरवणी मागण्यांद्वारे या निधीची तरतूद केल्याने नाशिक जिल्हा बँकेला मोठा आर्थिक आधार मिळाला असून, बँकेच्या पुनरुज्जीवनाचा मार्ग प्रशस्त झाला आहे.

title: মায়ঙত সন্ধানহীন এজন যুৱক...
হলৌ উঠিল টকৌ গছতঃ নহয়, অখিল উঠিল লেতেকু গছত...
description:
ডিজিটেল ডেস্কঃ  সাধাৰণতে অসমৰ সামাজিক সমস্যা, ৰাজনৈতিক বাক-বিতণ্ডাক লৈ ব্যস্ত হৈ থাকে কৃষক নেতা অখিল গগৈ। শিৱসাগৰ সমষ্টিৰ বিধায়কগৰাকীৰ সেই সক্ৰিয়তাই ব্যাপক চৰ্চা লাভ কৰি আহিছে।  সততে তেওঁ এইধৰণৰ ব্যক্তব্য, ভিডিঅ’ৱে সামাজিক মাধ্যমত মানুহৰ দৃষ্টি আকৰ্ষণ কৰে।
অতি সাধাৰণভাৱে জীৱন নিৰ্বাহ কৰা অখিল গগৈৰ শেহতীয়াকৈ আন এক ভিডিঅ’ৱে এইবাৰ সামাজিক মাধ্যমত ধুমুহাৰ সৃষ্টি কৰিছে। বতৰৰ ফল খাবলৈ গৈ অখিল গগৈ উঠিল এজোপা লেতেকু গছত। বয়স হ’লেও অতি সহজতে হাফ পেন্ট আৰু টিচাৰ্ট পিন্ধি অখিল গগৈ উঠিল এজোপা লেতেকু গছত।
তাৰ পাছত গছৰ এটা ঠালিত বহি অখিল গগৈয়ে গছ ভৰি লাগি থকা লেতেকু ছিঙি খাই থকা দৃশ্য আৱদ্ধ কৰা হৈছিল কেমেৰাত। গছত উঠি লেতেকু খোৱা সেই ভিডিঅ’ অখিল গগৈয়ে আপলোড কৰিছিল তেওঁৰ ফেচবুক প্ৰফাইলত।
তাতেই নেটিজেন সকলে অতি তাতপৰ্যপূৰ্ণ মন্তব্য কৰা দেখা গৈছে। কোনোৱে অখিল গগৈৰ এই প্ৰতিভাক প্ৰশংসা কৰিছে আৰু আন কিছুৱে অখিল গগৈ এই কাৰ্যৰ সৈতে ৰাজনীতিক সংমিশ্ৰণ কৰি টিপ্পনী দিছে।
এজনে লিখিছে- মামা যদি গছে গছে, অখিল ডালে ডালে। আন এজনে লিখিছে- আমাৰ নেতাক কিহঁত বলে পাৰিব? এজনে আকৌ বিধায়ক অখিল গগৈৰ এই প্ৰতিভাক বান্দৰৰ সৈতে তুলনা কৰিছে। আন এগৰাকীয়ে লিখিছে- অখিল দা, চাব। আপোনাক দেখি মামাও গছ বগাবলৈ চাব।
কোনেৱে অখিল গগৈৰ গছ বগোৱা এই দৃশ্যটো দেখি শৈশৱলৈ ঘূৰি গৈছে। শৈশৱত এনেদৰে লেতেকু গছত বগাই লেতেকু খোলা স্মৃতি ৰোমন্থন কৰিবলৈ অৱকাশ পাইছে। লেতেকু গছ বগোৱা অখিলৰ এই দৃশ্যই গ্ৰাম্য জীৱনবোধৰ মাজত অতি সহজভাৱে জীৱন অতিবাহিত কৰা এজন নেতাক বিচাৰি পাইছে আন এচামে।
কমেন্টত অৱশ্যে এচামে অখিল গগৈক সমালোচনা কৰাও দেখা গৈছে। তেওঁৰ দৰে ব্যস্ত ৰাজনীতিবিদ এজনে সামাজিক মাধ্যমৰ সহায়ত মানুহৰ দৃষ্টি আকৰ্ষণ কৰিবলৈ এইবোৰ তৰল কাম কৰি ভিডিঅ’ আপলোড দিয়া বুলিও মন্তব্য কৰিছে।

title: Indian IT firm’s another mega-AI deal: Persistent Systems to buy German firm Nagarro for €81 per share
description: Persistent Systems to buy Nagarro: The acquisition will combine Persistent's AI-led engineering and cloud capabilities with Nagarro's European digital engineering, ERP and customer experience expertise, creating a company with an annual revenue run rate of around USD 2.9 billion and a workforce of more than 46,000 employees across over 40 countries.
content: Persistent Systems to buy Nagarro: Pune-based Persistent Systems, a global IT services provider specialising in AI-driven digital engineering and enterprise modernisation, has agreed to acquire Germany’s Nagarro SE, a digital engineering firm, in an all-cash deal.This marks one of the largest overseas acquisitions by an Indian IT services company.Persistent to buy Nagarro SE for €81 per sharePersistent Systems has announced a voluntary public takeover offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Nagarro SE through its subsidiary Galaxy Germany Holding for €81 per share in cash.The proposed acquisition will combine Persistent's AI-led engineering and cloud capabilities with Nagarro's European digital engineering, ERP and customer experience expertise, creating a company with an annual revenue run rate of around USD 2.9 billion and a workforce of more than 46,000 employees across over 40 countries.What does the offer represent?The offer represents a 140 per cent premium to Nagarro's undisturbed closing share price on June 25 and a 94 per cent premium to its three-month volume-weighted average price. The deal is expected to close by Q4CY26 or Q1CY27.Persistent has already secured the backing of Nagarro's largest shareholder, Lantano Beteiligungen GmbH, which has agreed to tender its entire 21 percent stake in the company.Nagarro's management and supervisory boards have also backed the transaction and intend to recommend shareholders accept the offer, subject to reviewing the final offer document.Persistent bolsters European presence with this buyoutFor Persistent, the acquisition significantly strengthens its European presence. Post-transaction, Europe will account for about 22 per cent of the combined company's revenue, up from 9 percent currently, while North America will continue to contribute around 62 per cent.As a combined group, they will have an expanded total Addressable Market (TAM) to over $1,400 billion, across 350 plus client relationships.“AI is reshaping our industry at an unprecedented pace. Success will belong to companies that combine deep technical capability with global reach, while continuing to attract, develop and inspire exceptional people. Together, Persistent and Nagarro will be better positioned to help our clients navigate this new era, creategreater opportunities for our teams, and build an organisation that will endure for many years to come,” said Anand Deshpande, founder, chairman and managing director, Persistent Systems.Persistent Systems Q4 resultsPersistent Systems has reported a 33.73 per cent growth in consolidated net profit to Rs 529.26 crore in the January-March quarter of FY26.It had posted a net profit of Rs 395.76 crore in the same period last fiscal, according to regulatory filings.The company's revenue from operations increased by about 25 per cent to Rs 4,055.93 crore in Q4 FY26, as compared to Rs 3,242.11 crore in Q4 FY26.Persistent Systems noted a statutory impact of Rs 89 crore on account of the implementation of the new labour codes.Sequentially, profit and revenue rose by 20.43 per cent and 7.35 per cent over the previous quarter (October-December), respectively.

title: केंद्रीय मंत्रिमंडळ विस्तारात शिंदे गटाच्या ‘या’ खासदारांना लागू शकते लॉटरी, कॅबिनेटसह राज्यमंत्रीपद मिळू शकते
description: मुंबई | Mumbaiपंतप्रधान नरेंद्र मोदी यांच्या नेतृत्वातील एनडीए सरकारचा लवकरच मंत्रिमंडळ विस्तार होण्याची शक्यता असून, त्यात शिंदेसेनेला वाढीव प्रतिनिधित्व दिले जाईल. शिवसेना शिंदे गटातर्फे खा. डॉ. श्रीकांत शिंदे, परभणीचे खासदार संजय जाधव किंवा धाराशिवचे खासदार ओमराजे निंबाळकर यांच्या पैकी एकाला किंवा दोघांना ही संधी दिली जाण्याची शक्यता आहे. ‘ऑपरेशन टायगर’च्या यशानंतर केंद्रीय मंत्रिमंडळात शिवसेनेला एक […]
content:
मुंबई | Mumbaiपंतप्रधान नरेंद्र मोदी यांच्या नेतृत्वातील एनडीए सरकारचा लवकरच मंत्रिमंडळ विस्तार होण्याची शक्यता असून, त्यात शिंदेसेनेला वाढीव प्रतिनिधित्व दिले जाईल. शिवसेना शिंदे गटातर्फे खा. डॉ. श्रीकांत शिंदे, परभणीचे खासदार संजय जाधव किंवा धाराशिवचे खासदार ओमराजे निंबाळकर यांच्या पैकी एकाला किंवा दोघांना ही संधी दिली जाण्याची शक्यता आहे.



‘ऑपरेशन टायगर’च्या यशानंतर केंद्रीय मंत्रिमंडळात शिवसेनेला एक कॅबिनेट मंत्रीपद मिळण्याची शक्यता आहे. कॅबिनेट मंत्रीपदासाठी शिंदेंच्या शिवसेनेकडून एकनाथ शिंदे यांचे पुत्र आणि कल्याणचे खासदार डॉ. श्रीकांत शिंदे यांच्या नावावर जवळपास शिक्कामोर्तब झाल्याचे समजले जात आहे. तर शिंदेसेनेला एकूण तीन मंत्रिपदे मिळाली तर प्रतापराव जाधव यांना कायम ठेवूनही आणखी दोन मंत्रिपदे मिळतील. अशावेळी श्रीकांत शिंदे आणि संजय जाधव किंवा ओमराजे निंबाळकर यांच्यापैकी एकास संधी मिळू शकेल.



संपादकीय : २७ जून २०२६ – कळ्या खुडणारा ‘शहरी शहाणपणा’



प्रतापराव जाधव यांचं मंत्रिपद जाणार की राहणार?सध्याचे आयुष खात्याचा स्वतंत्र प्रभार असलेले मंत्री प्रतापराव जाधव यांच्याऐवजी खासदार संजय जाधव यांना संधी मिळण्याची शक्यता आहे. प्रतापराव जाधव यांच्याकडे आयुष खात्याच्या स्वतंत्र प्रभाराबरोबरच आरोग्य खात्याच्या राज्यमंत्री पदाची जबाबदारी आहे. प्रतापराव जाधव यांच्या कामकाजाबद्दल केंद्रात नाराजी असल्याची सूत्रांची माहिती आहे. त्यामुळे संभाव्य मंत्रिमंडळ विस्तारात प्रतापराव जाधव यांचे मंत्रीपद जाणार की राहणार? याबद्दल प्रचंड चर्चा सुरु आहे.



डॉ. श्रीकांत शिंदेंनी घेतले तिरुपती बालाजीचं दर्शनपुढच्या काही दिवसात केंद्रीय कॅबिनेटचा विस्तार होणार असल्याची चर्चा आहे. त्याआधी खासदार श्रीकांत शिंदे आणि ओमराजे निंबाळकरांनी तिरुपती बालाजीचं दर्शन घेतलं. दोघांनीही चार किलोमीटर पायी चालत दर्शन घेतलंय. त्यामुळे या दोघांची नावे चर्चेत आहेत. ठाकरेंचे 6 बंडखोर शिवसेनेत सामील झाल्यानं शिवसेनेच्या खासदारांची संख्या 13 वर गेलीय. यामुळे खासदारांची सर्वाधिक संख्या असणारा शिवसेना हा महाराष्ट्रातील सर्वात मोठा पक्ष ठरला आहे.



अजित पवार गटाला मंत्रिपद मिळणार?महायुतीतील तिसरा महत्वाचा पक्ष असलेल्या राष्ट्रवादीच्या अजित पवार गटाचे सुनील तटकरे हे एकमेव खासदार आहेत. ज्येष्ठ नेते प्रफुल्ल पटेल, पार्थ पवार आणि नितीन पाटील हे राज्यसभा सदस्य आहेत. या पक्षाला केंद्रात एकच आणि तेही राज्यमंत्रिपद मिळाले तर पार्थ पवार यांचे नाव नक्की होऊ शकते. सुनील तटकरे यांच्या नावावरही विचार होऊ शकतो. मात्र, कॅबिनेट मंत्रिपद मिळाले तर प्रफुल्ल पटेलांस हे तिघेही दावेदार असतील. सध्या अजित पवार गटाकडे एकही केंद्रीय मंत्रिपद नाही.

title: ঢকুৱাখনাত উন্নয়নৰ ফোঁপোলা স্বৰূপঃ টুলুঙা নাৱেৰে বিপদসংকুল যাত্ৰা
Why every cocktail menu suddenly looks like science experiment: The rise of lab-inspired mixology and molecular drinking
description: Cocktail culture is undergoing a scientific transformation as bartenders embrace molecular mixology, fermentation, clarification and local ingredients. Industry experts reveal why lab-inspired drinks, theatrical presentations and flavour-focused innovation are reshaping bar menus and consumer experiences across India.
content: I still remember a time when ordering a cocktail was fairly straightforward. You picked a classic Old Fashioned, perhaps a Martini, maybe something fruity if you were feeling adventurous. The bartender shook, stirred, poured and garnished. End of story. Today, however, ordering a cocktail often feels like stepping into a chemistry laboratory. A glass arrives engulfed in smoke. Another comes topped with an impossibly smooth foam. One is crystal clear despite containing ingredients that should make it cloudy. Somewhere, a bartender is using a dropper, a centrifuge or a vacuum-sealing machine. Suddenly, drinks menus read less like bar lists and more like scientific journals. And honestly, I am fascinated. Cocktails are no longer judged solely by how they taste. The visual experience, the presentation, the story behind the ingredients and even the preparation process have become equally important. Across India's leading bars, mixology is undergoing a transformation—one that blends science, creativity and theatre in a way we've never seen before.           Why are cocktails becoming more theatrical? The answer lies partly in changing consumer expectations. As social media has transformed dining and drinking culture, people increasingly seek experiences worth sharing. A cocktail today isn't merely a beverage; it is content, conversation and entertainment rolled into one. Mr. Michael D'Souza, Master Distiller at Paul John Whisky, believes this shift is rooted in the modern consumer's desire for memorable experiences. "Consumers are increasingly seeking memorable experiences. Today, going out for cocktails is as much about the experience as it is about the drink itself. A beautifully presented cocktail with smoke swirling around it or a dramatic tableside serve becomes the integral part of the evening's entertainment. It gives the consumer something to talk about—and, of course, something to share and post on." The result is a new generation of drinks designed to surprise before they even touch your lips. Is molecular mixology still about smoke and foams? Not quite. While molecular mixology first gained popularity through dramatic presentations involving dry ice, edible bubbles and unusual textures, industry experts say the science has evolved. Ajay Nayyar, Lead, Bartender Advocacy, Diageo India, points out that today's bars are increasingly borrowing techniques from professional kitchens rather than relying purely on visual theatrics. "A thought that may be worth exploring is that today's cocktail bars are increasingly adopting techniques that were once associated with professional kitchens and laboratories. While molecular mixology was once defined by smoke, foams and visual theatrics, the science has now moved behind the scenes." He adds, "From my experience with Diageo World Class, I've seen bartenders use clarification, fermentation, distillation and flavour extraction techniques to create entirely new drinking experiences. The focus is no longer on spectacle, but on creating cleaner flavours, greater consistency and deeper storytelling through ingredients." In other words, the science hasn't disappeared. It has simply become less visible. The rise of the invisible bartender-scientist Many of today's most impressive cocktails look deceptively simple. That crystal-clear highball on your table may have taken days to prepare. The seemingly effortless cocktail could involve multiple stages of fermentation, infusion and flavour extraction before it ever reaches the glass. Nayyar explains, "We've also introduced many bartenders to these concepts through initiatives with the TGCC Lab, particularly around fermentation and flavour development. What's fascinating is how these techniques are helping bartenders think beyond recipes and become flavour creators, finding new ways to express local ingredients, cultures and personal stories in a glass."           Perhaps his most compelling observation is this: "The irony is that while bars look more technical than ever, the drinks being served are often simpler than before. A clear highball or a minimalist cocktail may have days of preparation behind it. Perhaps the real story is not the rise of molecular mixology, but the rise of invisible science behind the bar." How Indian ingredients are changing the game One of the most exciting developments in modern mixology is India's growing influence on flavour creation. Rather than relying solely on imported ingredients, bartenders are increasingly exploring local produce, regional botanicals and indigenous flavours. According to Aryaan Sundaram, Brand Advocacy West – Monika Alcobev, what guests perceive as lab-inspired cocktails often begin with a culinary mindset. "What many guests see as "lab-inspired mixology" is really the result of bartenders taking a more culinary and research-driven approach to drinks." He explains, "Today's cocktail programs are drawing inspiration from professional kitchens, using techniques such as fermentation, sous-vide infusions, distillation, and fat-washing to create new flavors, improve consistency, and make better use of ingredients." The trend is especially visible in India's most innovative bars, where local ingredients are taking centre stage. "In India, this trend is also being shaped by a growing focus on indigenous produce. Bartenders today are foraging the most unique ingredients from different parts of the country, and even adding a personal touch with foraging ingredients from their hometowns, This helps bartenders to create cocktails that feel authentic and rooted in local culture." "Behind every modern cocktail menu is extensive experimentation, from ingredient sourcing and flavor development to sustainability and food pairing. What appears effortless in the glass is often the result of months of testing and refinement." Sundaram adds a personal perspective, "Whenever I go behind and represent Monika Alcobev behind the Bar, I love using different acid blends, in house pickles, cordials and techniques to try and create drinks that would elevate the experience for the consumer." Ultimately, he says, "Ultimately, the rise of lab-inspired mixology isn't about turning bars into laboratories. It's about combining creativity, technique, and local ingredients to create more distinctive and memorable drinking experiences."Why consumers are embracing these experimental drinks Of course, visual appeal alone cannot sustain a trend. The reason molecular cocktails continue to attract consumers is because they combine entertainment with flavour exploration. D'Souza explains, "Consumers are embracing theatrical, lab-inspired cocktails because they offer more than just a drink, they deliver an experience. Smoke, bubbles, foams, and interactive serves create excitement and make cocktails highly shareable on social media. However, while the spectacle attracts attention, flavour remains the deciding factor. Consumers may order a cocktail for the theatrics, but they'll only order it again if it tastes great. As a result, bars are increasingly balancing innovation with classic cocktail craftsmanship." That balance between innovation and taste appears to be driving the category's continued growth. Are we witnessing a lasting shift? Industry leaders believe the answer is yes. Kunal Madan, Chief Marketing Officer, Radico Khaitan, sees lab-inspired mixology as part of a broader evolution in premium drinking culture. "Lab-inspired mixology is taking cocktails beyond the usual glass-and-garnish routine. Today's consumers want drinks that feel immersive, memorable, and a little unexpected, it is like bringing together flavour, theatre, and craftsmanship all in one sip. While molecular techniques add a fun "mad scientist" vibe, the real focus is on elevating the spirit and creating premium, personalised experiences that people remember long after the ice melts." Rahul Mehra, Co-Founder & CEO, Third Eye Distillery, believes the movement is ultimately about discovery. "I think people sometimes mistake novelty for innovation. For years, the drinks industry sold consumers familiarity. Today, consumers are rewarding discovery. That's what's driving interest in techniques like clarification, carbonation and molecular preparation. Not because people care about the science, but because they're looking for something genuinely different."           He continues, "The best bartenders understand that innovation isn't about making drinks more complicated. It's about making flavour more interesting. What's exciting in India is seeing global techniques used to express local ingredients and flavours. That's where originality lives." And perhaps most importantly, "Consumers can tell when something is innovative and when it's simply performative. The spectacle may start the conversation, but nobody orders a second drink because it was clever. They order it because it was good." So, what comes next? The future of cocktails may actually be less dramatic than many imagine. According to D'Souza, the industry's next phase will focus on meaningful innovation rather than visual gimmicks. "I think it's here to stay, but in a more refined form. The flashy smoke and bubbles may come and go, but the techniques behind lab-inspired mixology, like clarification, fat-washing, and advanced infusions, are becoming part of the modern bartender's toolkit. Interestingly, the future of cocktails may be less about smoke and spectacle and more about sustainability, local ingredients, fermentation, and creative low-alcohol serves. The science isn't going away, it’s just becoming more refined and purposeful." And perhaps that's the most fascinating part of all. The cocktails that look like science experiments today are not really about science. They are about storytelling, craftsmanship and flavour. The laboratory aesthetic may grab our attention, but behind every puff of smoke and crystal-clear drink lies something far more important: a bartender's relentless pursuit of making a better cocktail. The beakers, bubbles and test tubes are merely the opening act.

title: Man dies after airbag deploys inside parked car in Thane
description:
Thane, Jun 27: In a bizarre accident, a 25-year-old man bled to death after an airbag unexpectedly deployed inside a parked vehicle in Maharashtra's Thane district, police said on Saturday.
The incident occurred in the Kashimira area on Wednesday when the victim, a car dealer, was inside a 15-year-old car, and the safety system suddenly triggered, an official said.
The airbag deployed, and the massive impact left him with severe injuries, leading to fatal blood loss before he could receive medical aid.
According to a preliminary probe, despite its age, official records indicate that the car still held a valid fitness certificate, the official said, adding that the police have registered a case of accidental death and have launched an investigation and are consulting automobile experts.

title: US Releases Footage of Airstrikes on Iran | WATCH
description: The US Central Command released a brief video demonstrating strikes against Iran, which were a response to an attack on a commercial vessel.
content: The US Central Command (CENTCOM) on Friday released a 37-second video showing American forces carrying out strikes against Iran, describing the operation as a response to Tehran's attack on a commercial cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. The strikes came a day after Iran allegedly attacked the Singapore-flagged cargo ship M/V Ever Lovely with a one-way attack drone while the vessel was exiting the Strait of Hormuz along the Omani coast. In a statement accompanying the video, CENTCOM said the attack on the commercial ship was an "unwarranted aggression" that violated the ceasefire and threatened freedom of navigation through one of the world's most critical maritime trade routes."US aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations and coastal radar sites after Iran hit M/V Ever Lovely on June 25 with a one-way attack drone," CENTCOM said.It added that American forces continue to coordinate and support the safe passage of commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz."The US military remains present and vigilant to ensure all aspects of the agreement with Iran are adhered to, obeyed, and in full force and effect," the statement said.The 37-second footage released by CENTCOM shows precision strikes carried out, though no further operational details or the locations of the targets were disclosed.Iran retaliatesAfter the United States launched airstrikes on Iranian military targets, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy announced that it had targeted US military positions in the region in retaliation, IRNA News Agency reported on Friday. Ebrahim Azizi, a member of the Islamic Consultative Assembly of Iran, said that the US does not commit to a ceasefire, and the blame game does not work anymore.In a post on X, he said, "The U.S. attacked Iran in the middle of negotiations once again. The failed U.S. President has shown he has no commitment to the principles of negotiation or a ceasefire. This reckless violation of the ceasefire will, as always, lead to retreat and regret on their part. The blame game does not work anymore."The latest exchange comes only days after Washington and Tehran agreed to halt hostilities following weeks of military confrontation.(With agency inputs)

title: Munchies, Multivitamins and Make-Up Have No Expiry Date– Biggest Concerns Answer...
description: Deceiving to sell:  No manufacturing or expiry date on the products you eat and apply? Times Now Digital's "E-Comms Hiding Health Info" campaign takes up the fight, with legal experts addressing your biggest concerns on remedies, refunds and platform liability.
content: Deceiving to sell: When you pick a product off a shop shelf, you turn it over and check one thing first: when was it made, and when does it die? On India's quick-commerce apps, that box is simply missing. No manufacturing date. No expiry. Tap, pay, delivered in ten minutes, and you have no idea what you're actually eating, swallowing or putting on your skin.Times Now Digital has launched a campaign — "E-Comms Hiding Health Info" — to hold these platforms accountable for burying information you are legally entitled to see.The pattern is hard to miss. As ten-minute delivery exploded after Covid, the regulatory check at the door seems to have gone 'quick' too — quick to approve, quick to skip the basics. Missing manufacturing and expiry dates say exactly that. And the concern is no longer theoretical: cases are piling up across food, cosmetics and health products sold online.We asked the biggest names in the business – Blinkit, Zepto, Swiggy Instamart, BigBasket, Amazon Now and Flipkart Minutes – a simple question: why are manufacturing and expiry dates not on your listings? Every one of them stayed silent. Blinkit and Amazon asked for 48 hours. We gave them another 48.    Still not a word, as of June 27.But here's what the apps won't tell you: whether you shop in a store or on a screen, your rights don't shrink. Here's what the law actually gives you.Q: Do these platforms even have a legal duty to show this information?According to legal professionals, Indian consumer protection and product-labelling laws require online marketplaces to display essential product details that help buyers make informed decisions, particularly for food products, cosmetics, health supplements, and other goods carrying shelf-life declarations.Varun Katiyar, Managing Partner at Consortium Legal, told Times Now Digital: "In the online marketplace, this responsibility becomes even more important because consumers cannot physically inspect products before buying them. Non-disclosure of such information can be viewed as a failure of transparency and may amount to an unfair trade practice or deficiency in service under consumer protection laws."Abhishek Bagga, Associate Partner, King Stubb & Kasiva, added: "Under the Legal Metrology (Packaged Commodities) Rules 2011, read with the Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules 2020, consumers are entitled to be provided with all mandatory product information before deciding whether to make a purchase. Where a product is legally required to carry an expiry, best before or use by declaration such as food products, health supplements and several cosmetic products that information must ordinarily be displayed on the online product listing."Q: A product arrives expired. What can you actually do about it?Experts noted that consumers who discover that a product is expired or nearing expiry after delivery can seek refunds, replacements, or other remedies through the seller or platform's grievance redressal mechanism.If complaints remain unresolved, consumers can approach Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019. Remedies may include refunds, compensation for losses or inconvenience, and directions against unfair trade practices.Bagga added that consumers can also raise complaints through the National Consumer Helpline or the INGRAM portal. Depending on the nature of the product, complaints may be filed with the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA), Legal Metrology authorities, or the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI).In cases where expired products cause illness, injury, or financial loss, consumers may also pursue product liability claims under the Consumer Protection Act.    Q: Who's on the hook — the seller, the app, or both?While the seller and manufacturer bear primary responsibility for ensuring compliance with product safety and labelling requirements, experts said e-commerce platforms cannot completely distance themselves from such violations.Varun said the extent of liability would depend on the platform's role — whether it was merely providing a marketplace or had knowledge of violations and failed to take corrective action.Decoding it further, Abhishek said that under the Legal Metrology (Packaged Commodities) Rules, the e-commerce platform has an independent obligation to display mandatory declarations, including the expiry or best-before date wherever applicable. A failure to comply with that obligation may expose the platform to liability separate from that of the seller.Q: You've complained again and again. They keep ignoring it. Now what?Persistent failure to disclose mandatory expiry information is not merely a customer service issue — it is a potential violation of consumer protection and product labelling laws. Such non-compliance can expose both the seller and, in appropriate cases, the e-commerce platform to consumer proceedings as well as regulatory action.Consumers can approach Consumer Commissions alleging deficiency in service and unfair trade practices if repeated complaints are ignored. Authorities such as the CCPA, Legal Metrology departments, and FSSAI may also intervene in cases involving systemic non-compliance, they said.A date on a label is not a courtesy. It is the law, and it is the difference between a product that's safe and one that's quietly past its end. The apps built their business on speed. They don't get to outrun the basics. Times Now Digital will keep asking until they answer.

title: Death toll rises to 589; over 2900 people injured after twin earthquakes in Venezuela
description:
Venezuela, June 27: The death toll following the fatal earthquakes in Venezuela has crossed 500 while over 2900 people have been injured, acting President Delcy Rodriguez told state broadcaster VTV, according to CNN.
As per the report by CNN, Rodriguez said on Friday that the death toll has risen to 589, with 2,980 people injured.
The update comes as rescue operations are underway to bring out people trapped under the rubble during the critical "golden window" of up to 72 hours following the earthquake.
CNN reported that the death toll is expected to rise significantly as search teams find more victims.
"We haven't slept a wink in our efforts to save lives," Rodriguez told VTV, as she also praised the arrival of international assistance and emergency crews. "We have saved dozens of lives."
As per VTV, search and rescue teams are concentrating efforts on the state of La Guaira, which appears to be the most affected area following the deadly back-to-back earthquakes.
It was reported that the government said it has also established a stockpile centre of food, water and medicines for those in need at the foreign ministry in Caracas.
Humanitarian efforts are underway in Venezuela as hundreds of citizens have volunteered to deliver water, food, medicines, and supplies to the affected communities in Caraballeda and Vargas.
India has launched 'Operation Amistad' to support earthquake-hit Venezuela, with two Indian Air Force C-17 aircraft carrying a field hospital unit and more than 35 tonnes of relief supplies, External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar announced on Friday.
In a post on X, announcing the operation, Jaishankar said, "Operation Amistad underway! Two @IAF_MCC C17S took off today for Venezuela with urgent assistance to support their post-earthquake relief efforts."
The relief mission includes an Indian Army Field Hospital Unit and over 35 tonnes of humanitarian assistance, including medicines, medical equipment and two BHISHM Cubes, designed to provide rapid emergency medical support in disaster situations. (ANI)

PM Modi emplanes for two-day state visit to Seychelles, to attend National Day Golden Jubilee celebrations
description:
New Delhi , June 27: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday embarked for Victoria, Seychelles, where he will undertake a two-day State Visit to participate in the Golden Jubilee celebrations of the country's National Day as the Guest of Honour.
"Leaving for Seychelles, where I will take part in their National Day celebrations. This year, it is even more special because it is the Golden Jubilee celebrations. Seychelles is a valued maritime neighbour and a key partner in our Vision MAHASAGAR, as well as in advancing the interests of the Global South. I look forward to discussions with my friend, Dr. Patrick Herminie, President of the Republic of Seychelles. I am honoured to be addressing the National Assembly of Seychelles during the visit. I also look forward to meeting the Indian community there," the PM posted on X ahead of his departure.
The Prime Minister's visit from June 27 to June 29 comes at the invitation of Seychelles President Patrick Herminie and is aimed at further strengthening the longstanding partnership between the two countries.
Ahead of his departure, PM Modi said Seychelles remains a valued maritime neighbour and a key partner in India's Vision MAHASAGAR and its shared commitment to the Global South.
He stated that the visit would provide an opportunity for both countries to further deepen their longstanding partnership and work together to advance the progress of their people while promoting security and prosperity in the Indian Ocean region.
"Building on the successful State visit of President Herminie to India in February 2026, I look forward to our discussions aimed at further strengthening our enduring friendship. Together, we will work to advance the progress of our peoples and promote security and prosperity in the Indian Ocean region," the Prime Minister said in his departure statement.
During the visit, PM Modi will hold talks with President Herminie to review the full spectrum of bilateral cooperation and exchange views on regional and international issues of mutual interest.
The Prime Minister will also become the first Indian Prime Minister to address the National Assembly of Seychelles, besides interacting with members of the Indian diaspora in the island nation.
The visit holds special significance as India and Seychelles are marking the 50th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations, which are rooted in mutual trust, shared democratic values, respect for diversity and deep people-to-people ties.
A contingent of the Indian Armed Forces, along with two Indian Navy ships, will also participate in the Golden Jubilee National Day celebrations, highlighting the close defence and maritime cooperation between the two countries.
PM Modi's visit is expected to further deepen bilateral ties, enhance maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean region and reinforce the shared vision of a secure, peaceful and prosperous region. (ANI)
